summary: Irish modernist author of Ulysses, Dubliners, A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man, and Finnegans Wake. Revolutionized prose style.

works.name: Dubliners
works.description: A collection of fifteen stories arranged from young to old protagonists. 'Araby': narrator fails to buy a gift for Mangan's sister at the title bazaar, sees himself as 'a creature driven and derided by vanity.' 'Eveline': title character clutches an iron railing, decides against immigrating to Buenos Aires with Frank. 'The Dead': Gabriel Conroy learns about Gretta's deceased former lover Michael Furey after Bartell D'Arcy sings 'The Lass of Aughrim'; ends with snow falling 'upon all the living and the dead.' 'A Painful Case': Mr. Duffy reads of Emily Sinico's death by train. 'Two Gallants': Lenehan dines on peas and ginger beer. 'Clay': spinster from Lamplight laundry touches a wet lump while blindfolded. 'A Mother': amateur impresario with a 'game leg.' 'An Encounter': boys ditch school, meet a man who rants about whipping boys. 'The Sisters': Father Flynn's death. 'A Little Cloud': Little Chandler's failed literary ambitions.

clues.clue: 'Araby': fails to buy gift for Mangan's sister at bazaar; 'creature driven and derided by vanity'
examples: sees himself as 'a creature driven and derided by vanity' after failing to obtain a gift for Mangan's sister at the title bazaar

clues.clue: 'The Dead': Bartell D'Arcy sings 'The Lass of Aughrim'; Gretta remembers Michael Furey; snow
examples: Bartell D'Arcy sings 'The Lass of Aughrim' and Gretta Conroy is reminded of Michael Furey

clues.clue: 'Eveline': clutches iron railing, decides against Buenos Aires
examples: a girl clutches an iron railing and looks at her lover with 'no sign of love or farewell or recognition'

clues.clue: 'A Painful Case': Mr. Duffy reads of Emily Sinico's death by train
examples: Emily Sinico being run over by a train, which Mr. Duffy reads after breaking off their Platonic affair

clues.clue: 'Clay': spinster from Lamplight laundry touches wet lump
examples: a spinster who works at the 'Lamplight laundry' touches a prayer book and a wet lump of the title substance

works.name: Ulysses
works.description: Set nearly entirely on June 16, 1904 (Bloomsday, inspired by Joyce's first date with Nora Barnacle). Opens with 'stately, plump Buck Mulligan.' Stephen Dedalus cries 'Non serviam!' and strikes a chandelier with his ashplant after seeing his mother's ghost in the 'Circe' chapter (set in Nighttown brothel owned by Bella Cohen). 'Oxen of the Sun' recapitulates the history of English writing as Mina Purefoy gives birth. Stephen calls God 'a shout in the street' and history 'a nightmare from which I am trying to awake.' Molly Bloom's soliloquy ends 'yes I said yes I will Yes.' The 'ineluctable modality of the visible.' Stephen corrects a dean's use of 'funnel' to 'tundish.' A commemorative coin added the word 'that' erroneously. Rebecca West wrote the novel was 'ugly and incompetent' but 'a work of art.' The Ulysses obscenity trials: first trial convicted Anderson and Heap of The Little Review for the 'Nausicaa' episode; Judge Woolsey's opinion about 'old Saxon words' was published in later editions. Tom Stoppard's Travesties involves Joyce and Henry Carr feuding over a staging of Wilde's Earnest. Richard Ellmann wrote the definitive biography.

clues.clue: Set on June 16 (Bloomsday); Molly Bloom's soliloquy ends 'yes I said yes I will Yes'
examples: a novel set nearly entirely on June 16, 1904
concludes with a soliloquy ending 'yes I said yes I will yes'

clues.clue: Opens with 'stately, plump Buck Mulligan'; based on friend Oliver St. John Gogarty
examples: Ulysses opens by describing a 'stately, plump' character with this surname

clues.clue: 'Circe' chapter in Nighttown; Stephen sees mother's ghost; 'Non serviam!'; Bella Cohen's brothel
examples: cries out 'Non serviam!' and strikes a chandelier with his ashplant
Stage directions introduce a brothel in Nighttown owned by Bella Cohen

clues.clue: 'Oxen of the Sun' recapitulates history of English prose; Mina Purefoy gives birth
examples: This author stylistically recapitulated the history of English writing in a chapter called 'Oxen of the Sun'

clues.clue: Stephen: God is 'a shout in the street'; history is 'a nightmare from which I am trying to awake'
examples: Stephen Dedalus describes God as a 'shout in the street' and history as a 'nightmare'

clues.clue: Obscenity trials: Anderson and Heap convicted for 'Nausicaa'; Woolsey's 'old Saxon words' opinion
examples: ruled against lesbian editors Anderson and Heap of The Little Review based on the Hicklin test

works.name: Finnegans Wake
works.description: Begins mid-sentence with 'riverrun, past Eve and Adam's'; last line forms a sentence with the first. Hundred-letter 'thunder words.' Anna Livia Plurabelle (ALP) and H.C.E. (Humphrey Chimpden Earwicker). Coined the word 'quark.' Marshall McLuhan's War and Peace in the Global Village structured around the thunder words. Samuel Beckett's first essay, 'Dante... Bruno. Vico.. Joyce,' was written for the Exagmination of Work in Progress. Excerpts published in the journal transition.

clues.clue: 'riverrun, past Eve and Adam's'; circular structure; hundred-letter thunder words; coined 'quark'
examples: begins mid-sentence with the phrase 'riverrun, past Eve and Adam's'
combined words for thunder in several languages to construct hundred-letter 'thunder words'

clues.clue: ALP = Anna Livia Plurabelle; HCE; washerwomen gossip
examples: The initials ALP refer to Anna Livia Plurabelle
framed as a dialogue filled with river names between two women doing laundry

works.name: A Portrait of the Artist as a Young Man
works.description: Autobiographical novel about Stephen Dedalus who attends Jesuit schools (Clongowes Wood and Belvedere). Father Arnall's terrifying hell sermon traumatizes Stephen. Stephen corrects a dean's use of 'funnel' to 'tundish.' He vows to forge 'the uncreated conscience of my race.' Inspired Clarice Lispector's Near to the Wild Heart.

clues.clue: Stephen Dedalus attends Jesuit schools; Father Arnall's hell sermon; 'Non serviam'; forge 'uncreated conscience of my race'
examples: writes his intention to forge 'the uncreated conscience of my race'
Father Arnall's sermon appears in this novel about the early life of Stephen Dedalus

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: Joyce's wife Nora Barnacle inspired Gretta Conroy and the character who works at Finn's Hotel. Their first date (June 16) became Bloomsday. Joyce's infamously filthy love letters to Nora sold for 240,000 pounds at Sotheby's in 2004. Daughter Lucia Joyce danced at Paris's inaugural international dance festival, wore a fish-scale costume, was institutionalized in 1936. Joyce's play Exiles involves Richard Rowan and Robert Hand in Dublin; inspired by his affair with Nora in Trieste. Pomes Penyeach collection includes 'Bahnhofstrasse.' Joyce wrote for/about the journal transition. Tom Stoppard's Travesties depicts Joyce feuding with Henry Carr. In Flann O'Brien's The Dalkey Archive, Joyce fakes his death and works as a bartender.

clues.clue: Wife Nora Barnacle inspired Bloomsday (June 16); filthy love letters sold for 240,000 pounds
examples: The date of this person's first date with her eventual husband, June 16th, has become a holiday known as Bloomsday

clues.clue: Daughter Lucia institutionalized; danced in fish-scale costume; visited by Beckett
examples: wore a fish-scale costume at Paris's inaugural international dance festival

clues.clue: Play Exiles: Richard Rowan and Robert Hand in Dublin; inspired by Nora affair in Trieste
examples: Richard Rowan's relationship in this author's play Exiles was inspired by his affair during a stay in Trieste

clues.clue: Stoppard's Travesties: Joyce feuds with Henry Carr over Earnest staging
examples: This author and Henry Carr feud over a staging of Oscar Wilde's The Importance of Being Earnest in Tom Stoppard's Travesties

clues.clue: Flann O'Brien's Dalkey Archive: Joyce fakes death, works as bartender
examples: In a novel, Mick finds this author working in a bar after faking his death

comprehensive_summary:
James Joyce is an Irish modernist author whose works revolutionized 20th-century literature. Dubliners, his short story collection, is arranged from young to old protagonists. 'Araby' ends with the narrator seeing himself as 'a creature driven and derided by vanity.' 'The Dead' concludes with Gabriel Conroy contemplating Michael Furey's spirit as snow falls, after Bartell D'Arcy sings 'The Lass of Aughrim.' 'Eveline' clutches an iron railing. 'A Painful Case' centers on Mr. Duffy reading of Emily Sinico's death.

Ulysses is set on June 16, 1904 (Bloomsday, named for Joyce's first date with Nora Barnacle). It opens with Buck Mulligan and ends with Molly Bloom's 'yes I said yes I will Yes.' The 'Circe' chapter takes place in Nighttown where Stephen sees his mother's ghost. 'Oxen of the Sun' recapitulates English prose history. Stephen calls God 'a shout in the street.' The novel's obscenity trials included conviction of Anderson and Heap for the 'Nausicaa' episode and Judge Woolsey's landmark ruling.

Finnegans Wake begins with 'riverrun' and has a circular structure. It coined 'quark' and features hundred-letter thunder words. A Portrait of the Artist follows Stephen Dedalus through Jesuit schools with Father Arnall's hell sermon.

Joyce's love letters to Nora sold for 240,000 pounds. His daughter Lucia was a dancer institutionalized in 1936. His play Exiles features Richard Rowan. Stoppard's Travesties depicts Joyce feuding with Henry Carr. Richard Ellmann wrote the definitive biography.
